summary: On March 22, 2026, at approximately 10:48 p.m., the Alaska State Troopers responded to a residence on Old Elliott Highway for a report of a disturbance. The investigation determined Mulberger had unlawfully returned and attempted to contact the resident. Mulberger was arrested for Criminal Trespass in the Second Degree and transported to Fairbanks Correctional Center.

title: It's essential to fix this Permanent Fund oversight
summary: Legislative oversight was high on the list of priorities for the lawmakers who led the effort to get the Alaska Permanent Fund up and running after voters created the savings account 50 years ago. “We want insulation without isolation,” is how Rep. Clark Gruening put it. But a couple of years after the Alaska Permanent Fund Corporation came into existence, the Legislature abandoned its oversight duties. What had been identified in 1979-1980 as an urgent need for legislative review and monitoring—not just approving the annual budget—disappeared as legislators moved onto other priorities with oil prices, soaring then crashing.
